title: Acuff scores 30 as Calipari, Hogs roll to SEC title
content: Darius Acuff Jr. had 30 points as No. 17 Arkansas beat No. 22 Vanderbilt to win its first SEC tournament crown and first automatic NCAA tournament berth since 2000.

title: Sources: Goedert stays with Eagles on new deal
content: Tight end Dallas Goedert reached agreement Sunday on a one-year deal to return to the Eagles for his ninth season in Philadelphia, sources told ESPN's Adam Schefter.

title: Late flurry allows SGA to maintain 20-point run
content: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander barely extended his record streak of games with at least 20 points to 128 during Sunday's win over the Timberwolves.

title: Power drops 44 to lead Penn over Yale in Ivy final
content: TJ Power scored an Ivy Madness-record 44 points to lift Pennsylvania to an 88-84 victory over Yale to win the Ivy Legue championship on Sunday.

title: McDavid calls on NHL to audit suspension process
content: Connor McDavid called on the NHL to investigate how it doles out suspensions in the wake of a controversial ban of just five games for Radko Gudas' hit on Auston Matthews.

title: Vonn: 'I'm the only one that will decide my future'
content: Lindsey Vonn, who is recovering from a crash at the Olympics, said she's not ready to discuss her skiing future but "I'm the only one that will decide my future."

title: Para gold nets U.S. historic hockey sweep in Italy
content: Three weeks after the United States beat Canada in the Olympic hockey finals, the Americans overcame their neighbors again to win Paralympic gold and complete the three-peat at Milan Cortina.

title: DeChambeau wins playoff as foe misses 2-footer
content: Bryson DeChambeau hit his drive into the water and still won the playoff with a par at LIV Golf Singapore after Richard T. Lee of Canada missed a 2-foot putt.

title: ⚾ Big USA-Dominican Republic WBC showdown
content: Who will win Sunday's star-studded World Baseball Classic semifinal? We've got you covered with players to watch, live updates and postgame takeaways.

title: Has UCLA leapfrogged UConn for No. 1 overall and four more big questions for Selection Sunday
content: Where the top eight seeds play in the regionals is as big of a question as which bubble teams get in Selection Sunday.

title: Women's Bracketology: One final projection before the bracket is revealed
content: All 31 auto-bids are spoken for. Will UConn or UCLA be No. 1 overall? And where will the hosts be seeded?

title: Masterclass shows why Man Utd must keep Bruno Fernandes amid rebuild
content: Man United will lose Casemiro, and Bruno Fernandes could leave too -- but performance vs. Aston Villa reinforces the club must keep Fernandes.

title: NHL playoff watch: Projecting the Ducks' Pacific title chances
content: Here's how Anaheim can pull it off. Plus, full playoff projections, lottery standings after a busy Saturday.

title: Which NFL teams have improved the most in free agency? Five rosters that made the best moves
content: We picked out the teams that took the biggest steps forward this week through their signings and other moves -- including the 49ers.

title: Live tracking NFL free agency: Updates on signings, trades, cuts
content: We're following NFL free agency signings, trades, cuts, contract restructures and other moves as they happen.

title: Biggest questions facing Bears after free agent exodus
content: Chicago needs to figure out things at edge and in the secondary after a number of players left in free agency.

title: Steelers' offseason moves hint at Mike McCarthy's offensive plan
content: Pittsburgh's additions of Michael Pittman Jr. and Rico Dowdle portend a "physical, run-first offense" in 2026.

title: Chinese Grand Prix: Antonelli wins in Shanghai, Mercedes go 1-2 again - as it happened
content: Kimi Antonelli converted his record-breaking Chinese Grand Prix pole into a maiden Formula 1 victory on Sunday, you can relive exactly how it happened here.

title: WNBA CBA negotiations: New deal by Monday is goal as marathon meeting continues
content: The WNBA and union exchanged about 15 proposals between Tuesday and the early hours of Saturday morning.

title: 'I want to earn it': Tennessee track lands commit from Kevin Hart's son Hendrix
content: Hendrix Hart's personal bests include 10.68 seconds in the 100-meter dash and 21.35 in the 200-meter dash.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Real Madrid open to Camavinga exit, as Premier League clubs circle
content: Real Madrid are prepared to listen to offers from clubs looking to sign midfielder Eduardo Camavinga. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Cheat sheet: Sleepers, busts, rookies and closers
content: You've read their names all preseason long, it'd be a shame to forget them on draft day. The ESPN Cheat Sheet is one way to make sure that doesn't happen.

title: 'I'm what?' Netanyahu mocks death rumours, shows off five fingers while buying coffee - watch
content: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has publicly debunked circulating social media rumors about his death and a supposed "six fingers" claim.  He shared a video showcasing his five-fingered hands, dismissing the baseless allegations as fake news.  These false claims emerged amidst heightened regional tensions and online misinformation, with the PM's office confirming he is well.

title: Kharg Island: Trump's mercy shot on Iran's oil lifeline could reshape the war
content: The US has conducted airstrikes on military installations on Iran's Kharg Island, a vital oil export hub, while largely sparing oil infrastructure. President Trump cited "reasons of decency" but warned of reconsideration if shipping through the Strait of Hormuz is threatened. The move highlights the island's strategic importance for Iran's economy and regional security.

title: Calling it irrelevant, Judge to OpenAI lawyers: You can’t ask Musk about Ketamine use
content: A US judge has ruled that Elon Musk cannot be questioned about his ketamine use during his upcoming trial against OpenAI. The judge deemed the drug use irrelevant to the dispute over OpenAI's nonprofit structure, though limited questioning about Burning Man attendance may be allowed. The trial will focus on Musk's claims of being misled about OpenAI's nonprofit status.

title: FBI is investigating malware hidden in games on Steam: Suspected titles, safety tips for gamers and more
content: The FBI is investigating malware embedded in games on Steam, with a focus on potential victims between May 2024 and January 2026. Several games, including BlockBlasters and Chemia, have been identified as containing the malicious software. The agency is urging anyone who may have installed these games or has relevant information to come forward.

title: Shubman bags Polly Umrigar Award, Smriti Mandhana makes history with fifth BCCI honour
content: Shubman Gill and Smriti Mandhana were honored as India's top cricketers at the BCCI Naman Awards 2026. Gill secured the Polly Umrigar Award for his stellar 2025 season, including a remarkable Test series in England. Mandhana also shone, setting new ODI run-scoring records and playing a pivotal role in India's World Cup win.

title: Perplexity AI CEO agrees that Computer Science is gradually returning to its roots
content: AI is rapidly automating coding's routine tasks, pushing software engineering back towards its mathematical roots. Experts predict AI could soon handle most end-to-end coding, with some engineers already code-free. While LLMs still struggle with complex design, this shift is prompting a rethink in computer science education, emphasizing logic over syntax.

title: Iran crisis: IEA says strategic oil reserves to be released immediately in Asia-Oceania
content: Asia and Oceania will immediately receive strategic oil reserves, while the Americas and Europe will start releases by late March. This emergency action by the IEA aims to cushion the oil shock from the West Asia war, releasing 271.7 million barrels globally. Despite this, oil prices remain near $100, highlighting concerns over the Strait of Hormuz closure.

title: In poll-bound Assam, Shah plays Bangladesh infiltrators card
content: Union Home Minister Amit Shah accused Congress of aiding infiltrators in Assam and launched development projects worth over Rs 2,000 crore. He inaugurated healthcare facilities, including a medical college, and highlighted peace accords ending decades of militancy. Shah predicted a massive mandate for BJP, praising the state's improved healthcare and peace initiatives.

title: 'Expectations were immense': Dravid hails Surya & Gambhir in India’s T20 WC win
content: Rahul Dravid highlights India's consistent success in global tournaments. He credits this to the nation's immense cricketing talent finally being realized. Dravid noted the alignment of players, administrators, coaches, and fans. He also praised the T20 World Cup winning team for handling immense expectations and performing under pressure. This marks a significant period for Indian cricket.

title: NFL free agency: Titans keep Calvin Ridley; Trey Lance joins Chargers
content: The 2026 NFL free agency period has already produced major roster moves. Reports say the Tennessee Titans will keep Calvin Ridley on a restructured contract while quarterback Trey Lance is joining the Los Angeles Chargers. Other reported developments include Kyler Murray signing with the Vikings and the Ravens agreeing to a big deal with Trey Hendrickson after the Maxx Crosby trade collapsed.

title: Jordan Love’s Green Bay Packers linked to $321M ex-Minnesota Vikings QB
content: ESPN’s Bill Barnwell suggests Green Bay Packers as the perfect landing spot for veteran quarterback Kirk Cousins. With Jordan Love’s seldom absences, Cousins could serve as a trusted backup or bridge starter. His extensive experience and constant motivation to play at a high level make him a valuable gem for Green Bay. If he wants a Super Bowl before retirement, then the Packers’ the possible place.

title: Maria Sharapova, Luka Doncic photo goes viral after Lakers OT win vs Nuggets
content: Tennis icon Maria Sharapova made a surprise appearance at an NBA game and later posed for a photo with Luka Dončić after the Lakers’ thrilling overtime win. The photo quickly went viral online.

title: Iran claims 'Epstein-linked network' plotting ‘9/11‑like’ attack to frame Tehran
content: A top Iranian security official claims a network linked to Jeffrey Epstein is plotting a 9/11-style terrorist attack and intends to frame Iran. Ali Larijani stated Iran opposes such plots and has no conflict with the American people, amid escalating Middle East tensions.

title: Scheme to reduce chemical fertiliser use remains a ‘non-starter’
content: The government's PM-PRANAM scheme, designed to cut chemical fertiliser use, has failed to disburse any incentives to states over two and a half years. A parliamentary committee urges an urgent review to simplify eligibility and release due funds, as the scheme's impact window closes.

title: 'No team is bigger than the organisation': ICC chairman Jay Shah sends clear message
content: ICC Chairman Jay Shah emphasized that no team is bigger than the organization, reflecting on the recent T20 World Cup's success despite pre-tournament uncertainties. He highlighted shattered viewership records and congratulated associate teams for their strong performances. Shah also urged India's captain and coach to maintain their winning momentum and plan for future tournaments.

title: Kerala, Assam & Puducherry vote on April 9, TN on April 23, 2-phase polling in WB; results May 4
content: The Election Commission has unveiled the assembly poll schedule for West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Assam, Kerala, and Puducherry. Key political players like Mamata Banerjee, MK Stalin, and Himanta Biswa Sarma are gearing up for intense electoral battles across these states and the Union Territory.

title: 'In supreme interest of nation': RSS backs Centre's decisions amid Middle East turmoil
content: The RSS has called for an immediate end to the Middle East conflict, emphasizing global peace while backing the Indian government's actions as being in the nation's supreme interest.  General secretary Dattatreya Hosabale also addressed protests following a leader's killing, stressing peaceful expression and communal harmony. The organization reiterated its stance on Hindutva and its inclusive vision for Indians abroad.

title: Horoscope: Daily astrological predictions for March 16,
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for March 16, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: Why your brain struggles to finish this sentence after an hour of reels
content: Scientists warn that habitual short-form video consumption may be weakening crucial brain circuits for deep thinking and focus. Studies show a negative correlation between short-video addiction and prefrontal brain activity, impacting attention, impulse control, and self-regulation.

title: Gold, silver may see more corrective moves this week as Iran conflict drive volatility
content: Gold and silver prices are poised for continued volatility this week, influenced by Middle East conflict developments and key central bank policy announcements. Investors are closely watching for geopolitical shifts and forward guidance from the Fed, ECB, BoE, and PBOC. Despite recent price dips, analysts maintain that long-term allocation to bullion remains favoured due to its safe-haven and hedging properties.

title: West Indies players return home after travel disruption: CWI
content: After an arduous wait, the West Indies cricket team and its staff have successfully made their journey back home following their time in India for the T20 World Cup. The team faced significant challenges due to travel restrictions caused by escalating tensions in West Asia, resulting in closed airspace and canceled flights that kept them in Kolkata longer than anticipated.

title: DGCA temporarily eases pilot duty norms for Air India long-haul flights amid airspace curbs
content: Air India's long-haul flights face extended duty times due to Middle East airspace restrictions. The DGCA has granted temporary relaxations, extending flight duty limits by over an hour. However, allegations suggest Air India may have exceeded these new allowances on a Jeddah flight, highlighting the ongoing impact of regional conflict on aviation.

title: No Hitman! Virat Kohli picks Chris Gayle over Rohit Sharma as best T20 opener
content: Virat Kohli, in a recent 'This or That' challenge, named Chris Gayle as his top T20 opener over Rohit Sharma.  Kohli's selections progressed through Adam Gilchrist, Travis Head, and Virender Sehwag before reaching the final showdown. Gayle's prolific T20 record, including the highest individual IPL score, solidifies his legendary status in the format.

title: Kejriwal, Sisodia move SC after Delhi HC rejects plea to transfer excise policy case
content: Arvind Kejriwal and Manish Sisodia have approached the Supreme Court after the Delhi High Court Chief Justice rejected their plea to transfer the CBI's challenge to their discharge in the excise policy case. The appeal was to be heard by Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma, a decision the AAP leaders deemed lacking impartiality, citing early observations by the judge.

title: IEA’s record oil release may offer only limited relief if Hormuz stays shut: S&P
content: The International Energy Agency's historic 400 million barrel oil reserve release offers limited relief as the Strait of Hormuz remains closed. S&P Global Energy warns this move won't solve the core issue of disrupted shipping, leaving Asian markets particularly vulnerable.  Markets face continued imbalance until this critical chokepoint reopens.

title: PM Modi lauds Om Birla's leadership after no-trust motion fails; LS Speaker reacts
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi lauded Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla for his composure and impartiality following the rejection of a no-confidence motion against him. Modi commended Birla's adherence to parliamentary decorum and his affirmation that no one is above the rules, reinforcing democratic traditions.

title: 26 Elected To Rajya Sabha Unopposed. Big Fight In Three States
content: Twenty-six leaders have been elected unopposed in the Rajya Sabha election across seven states. The list includes big names

title: Arvind Kejriwal In Supreme Court, Challenges Decision In Excise Policy Case
content: On Monday, Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma is expected to hear the CBI's petition challenging the release of Arvind Kejriwal, his former Deputy Manish Sisodia and a few others accused in the excise policy case.

title: Iran Arrests Dozens Of People Accused Of 'Spying' For Israel
content: Dozens of people accused of sharing sensitive information with Israel have been arrested in several areas of Iran, local media reported on Sunday, as Israeli and US fighter jets continue to strike new targets in the country.

title: Iranian Women Footballers Who Sought Asylum In Australia To Return Home
content: Three members of the Iranian women's football team who had sought asylum in Australia have decided to return to Iran, the government said on Sunday.

title: Ladakh Groups Say Will Go Ahead With Shutdown Today To Demand Statehood
content: Several political and social groups in Ladakh will go ahead with a shutdown and protests today, even as the Centre revoked the National Security Act (NSA) against activist Sonam Wangchuk and ordered his release.

title: 17-Page Suicide Note By AIIMS Rajkot Student Leads To Arrest Of 5 Students
content: The investigation into the tragic death of a final-year MBBS student at AIIMS Rajkot has taken a significant turn. Following a detailed 17-page suicide note, the Gandhigram Police have arrested five fellow medical students.

title: 2026 Assembly Elections See Sharp Rise In Booths, Changing Voter Landscape
content: The Election Commission of India today announced the polling schedule for the 2026 Assembly elections in Assam, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, West Bengal and Puducherry.

title: "Win-Win" For Ladakh: Sonam Wangchuk's Wife On Revocation Of NSA
content: The Centre on Saturday revoked activist Sonam Wangchuk's detention, freeing him after 170 days of detention.

title: Will Himanta Sarma's Son Join Politics? What Assam Chief Minister Said
content: Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma has made it clear that he will not ask his son to join politics. The chief minister, also known as the BJP's strategist in the northeast, said his son is doing law and he expects him to do well in his field.

title: "Will Bring Hindu Leaders From Congress": Himanta Sarma Charts Path To Polls
content: "We will bring all Hindu leaders from the Congress to BJP," he said at an event today, claiming that 70 per cent of Congress leaders still follow him.

title: Election 2026 Date Highlights: Poll Dates For Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Assam, Puducherry Announced
content: The Election Commission announced dates for assembly polls in five states. Polls are due in West Bengal, Keralam, Tamil Nadu, Assam and Puducherry.

title: All About Key Constituencies, Star Candidates In Tamil Nadu Ahead Of Assembly Polls
content: With the EC announcing a single-phase election to 234 Assembly segments in Tamil Nadu on April 23, here is a look at the key constituencies and candidates.

title: All Eyes On Himanta Sarma, Gaurav Gogoi In Assam Assembly Polls
content: All eyes will be on Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma and his challenger and state Congress president Gaurav Gogoi in the upcoming assembly polls.

title: 'Our Problem Is With Illegal Bangladeshis': Himanta Sarma To NDTV
content: Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma has rubbished conspiracy theories behind why the Assam assembly election has been scheduled on April 9, ahead of the spring festival Bihu.

title: TVK's Vijay Is The New Kid On The Block, Will He Punch Above His Weight?
content: Actor Vijay's political entry, as his appearance in his films, was packed with suspense and expectations by a multitude of his followers.

title: "I Am Dead... For Coffee": Netanyahu Responds To Death Rumours In New Video
content: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu released a video on X after conspiracy theories circulated on social media claiming he had been killed in an Iranian strike.

title: Iran Fires 'Dancing Missile' For 1st Time In This War: All You Need To Know
content: Iran on Sunday launched a series of advanced missiles targeting Israeli and US positions, including the Sejjil missile, which has been used for the first time since the war began on February 28, Iranian news outlet PressTV reported.

title: NDTV Assam Power Play: Migration, Identity And Shifting Demographic Landscape
content: A debate on migration and identity in Assam unfolded during the NDTV Assam Power Play panel featuring BJP's Ashok Singhal, Congress's Pradyut Bordoloi and AIUDF's Hafiz Rafiqul Islam.

title: Video: Massive Tornado Ravages Odisha Town, 1 Dead, 70 Houses Damaged
content: A powerful Nor'wester tornado caused widespread destruction in Odisha's Mayurbhanj district on Sunday, damaging dozens of houses and leaving several people injured.

title: 'Babar Azam doesn't have a voice': Ex-Pak batter cites Kohli example in T20 WC rant
content: Former Pakistan batter Basit Ali criticized the decision to bat Babar Azam at No.4 during the T20 World Cup, suggesting he should have opposed the move like Virat Kohli. However, Kamran Akmal disagreed, stating players rarely challenge management, especially when out of form.

title: EC announces bypoll dates for 8 assembly constituencies across six states; check schedule
content: By-elections for eight assembly seats across six states, including Goa, Gujarat, Karnataka, Maharashtra, Nagaland, and Tripura, have been announced by the Election Commission. Polling dates are set for April 9 and April 23, with vote counting scheduled for May 4. These elections will fill vacant legislative seats, ensuring full representation in state assemblies.

title: Air India, AI Express to operate 48 flights connecting West Asia from today
content: The two airlines will have their scheduled operations to Jeddah and Muscat, including a total of 10 flights between Indian cities and Jeddah, a release said

title: DNT leaders move Supreme Court seeking distinct enumeration in 2027 Census
content: Petition seeks specific question on Denotified, Nomadic and Semi-Nomadic Tribes in houselisting and population enumeration phases of the 2027 Census

title: Over 30 vehicles stranded amid snowfall in J&K's Kishtwar; rescue operation under way
content: The vehicles got stuck due to fresh snowfall and slippery road conditions at the high-altitude passes, disrupting traffic movement on the road connecting Kishtwar in Jammu with south Kashmir's Anantnag district

title: U.S. attitude to the Kurdish question reflects colonial mindset, says Nilufer Koc
content: Spokesperson of Kurdistan National Congress says the U.S. made the proposal about Kurdish fighters without broader consultation with the Kurds

title: Tamil Nadu’s urban areas record higher reduction in number of voters than rural ones
content: Looking at the overall picture, urban constituencies in northern and western districts have experienced a perceptible decrease, whereas those in the central and southern regions have seen a moderate decline

title: DMK steps up seat-sharing discussions with allies
content: The talks between the DMK and the Left parties will resume on March 17; VCK leaders say at least 10 seats will justify the party’s strength and convince the cadre

title: T.N. Assembly election 2026: campaign window set at 38 days, same as in 2021
content: This period is crucial for parties contesting in alliances, as they will have to finalise seat-sharing numbers, identify constituencies, select candidates, file nominations, release manifestos, and draw up campaign itineraries

title: New FOB across railway track in Tiruvannamalai to be ready in April for public use
content: Officials of State Highways say that the new bridge will help commuters reach the railway station safely because at present, they have to cross the busy Chittoor - Cuddalore High Road

title: LPG bookings fall to 77 lakh; Indian crude tanker sails out of UAE’s Fujairah safely
content: All domestic ‘refineries are operating at high capacity and maintaining adequate crude oil inventories,’ the government says as Jag Laadki becomes the fourth Indian-flagged vessel to have come out of the war zone unharmed

title: Pak terrorist killed near LoC in Baramulla, says Army
content: The Army says “warlike stores” including an AK rifle, pistols and large amounts of ammunition have been recovered and the operation continues

title: Seven pilgrims from Tamil Nadu  killed, seven others injured in road accident in Nepal
content: A total of 24 pilgrims from Pollachi and Devakottai travelled to the Manakamana Temple in Gorkha district. The bodies are being taken to Kathmandu, from where they will be sent to Chennai, says a State government statement

title: Lands or homes forcibly seized from terror-affected families will be liberated, returned: J&K L-G Manoj Sinha
content: He hands over appointment letters to 27 kin, says pending cases will be reopened to hold perpetrators accountable

title: Parties welcome two-phase West Bengal polls; people will bring down TMC, says BJP
content: In 2021, the Assembly election in West Bengal was held in eight phases; in 2011 and 2016 there were six phases; the announcement for this year’s poll is a departure from the past

title: Ahead of Id, NC seeks release of Kashmiris booked under UAPA for protesting Khamenei killing
content: National Conference describes protest rallies as “mourning gatherings”, noting that Khamenei was not just a head of state, but a revered religious leader to many Kashmiris; MLAs say L-G promised to review cases ahead of Id

title: Hours before Rajya Sabha polling, AIMIM announces to vote for RJD candidate
content: NDA which is falling short of the 3 votes claiming to win all five seat whereas main opposition party RJD relying on five MLA of (AIMIM) and lone MLA of BSP.

title: Government response to West Asia conflict driven by supreme national interest: RSS
content: Stating that trade and commerce have to be done with people from across the globe, the RSS general secretary says the government is doing the “right” thing

title: Parties, outfits invoke ‘Brahmin’ angle to ‘pandit’ reference in U.P. police recruitment exam
content: A question asked candidates to choose a term for ‘a person who changes according to circumstances’. The options were ‘Pandit’ (scholar), ‘Avsarvadi’ (opportunist), ‘Nishkapat’ (innocent or sincere) and ‘Sadachari’ (virtuous). CM issues directions to avoid ‘derogatory’ and ‘disrespectful’ references

title: Diversify agriculture and encourage farmers to grow pulses, Supreme Court tells government
content: Besides a relook into policy and diversification of crops, the Supreme Court of India asks Centre to convene a meeting of stakeholders to look into various issues, including the absence of MSP for pulses

title: UPSC’s new rule: Supreme Court nod must for delay in choosing State DGPs
content: Attorney General tells Supreme Court that there is no “provision empowering the UPSC to condone such inordinate delay”; States cannot appoint acting DGP as there is no such concept, says top court

title: CBI arrests ‘recruiter’ linked to overseas cyber scam centres exploiting Indian nationals
content: The accused allegedly collected about $300-400 per head from jobseekers and facilitated their travel to Cambodia, where they were forced to carry out online frauds targeting victims in India

title: Over 900 chicken pox cases reported in Ernakulam in 2026
content: Across the State, 1,897 cases were reported this month alone, taking the total this year to 11,388, according to the Health department data

title: Hospitals canteens in Kochi struggling amid LPG crisis
content: Many fear that hospitals may soon be forced to scale back or even suspend certain operations if the situation persists

title: TDP issues show-cause notice to Eluru MP over drugs allegations
content: The party directs Putta Mahesh Kumar to submit explanation within five days and advises him to stay away from party activities pending review

title: PM lauds Speaker for conducting Lok Sabha proceedings with ‘dedication, patience and impartiality’
content: PM congratulates MPs for rejecting “political misconduct” by defeating the Opposition’s motion to remove the Speaker; says it’s a matter of concern when political disagreement turns into disrespect for parliamentary dignity

title: LDF declares 10 candidates for Kozhikode, UDF, NDA yet to announce nominees
content: CPI(M) is fielding all its incumbent MLAs, except in Koyilandy. Seat-sharing talks are reportedly in the final stages in the UDF and NDA.

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026 | protest builds against Ramya Haridas’ reported candidature in Chirayinkeezhu constituency
content: Protesters say that an “outsider” candidate should not be imposed on the constituency, seek someone  from Thiruvananthapuram.

title: On Viveka’s seventh death anniversary, his daughter seeks justice, picks holes in CBI probe
content: Expressing displeasure over the manner in which the CBI has conducted the probe, Suneetha Narreddy says the investigating agency could have dug deeper into the facts to arrive at a fair conclusion

title: A polling booth in Tamil Nadu stands out with just 5 voters
content: Polling officials will have to travel three hours through a difficult terrain to reach the booth to be set up at at Vellimalai in Varusanadu in Theni district

title: Assam inks settlement with Hmar, Kuki extremist groups
content: Deal envisages the formation of Hmar and Kuki welfare development councils; all four groups signed Suspension of Operations pact, and laid down arms in 2012; cadres will be rehabilitated, says Assam Minister

title: LDF steals a march on UDF by naming candidates within an hour of Kerala poll date announcement
content: LDF workers kick off roadshows after the CPI(M) and CPI name 106 candidates in back-to-back press conferences; Congress and IUML hold meeting to finalise the UDF’s seat-sharing arrangement

title: As Rahul Gandhi eyes Dalit consolidation through Kanshi Ram in U.P., BSP gets unsettled, political slugfest intensified
content: Rahul Gandhi claimed that if India’s first Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru had been alive at the time of Kanshi Ram’s rise, he would have been made Chief Minister from the Congress

title: Young scientists oppose proposal to raise retirement age to 65 in agricultural universities
content: They call for immediate recruitment to fill long-pending vacancies and create opportunities for young researchers

title: People’s forum stage protest march and hunger strike against ‘medical negligence’ in private hospital in Thrissur
content: Kallur native Rafi Jose had slipped into a coma following alleged medical negligence during treatment at Daya Hospital in Thrissur

title: Civic polls will be held in May, DKS tells ticket aspirants
content: The Deputy Chief Minister held a preparatory meeting with ticket aspirants and office-bearers for the GBA civic body elections at Bharat Jodo Bhavan in Bengaluru on Sunday (March 15); it was attended by over 950 aspirants and several city Congress leaders.

title: LPG crunch reaches Bengaluru clubs; banquets, takeaways curtailed
content: Bowring Institute, Bangalore Club, and Bangalore Golf Association have sent out similar circulars suspending certain services, while some, like Koramangala Club, have stopped taking bookings for major parties, prioritising services for previously booked events.

title: Election announcement puts pause on hopes of Sabari rail landowners
content: ‘The file regarding the setting up of land acquisition offices for the project is pending with the Revenue department, and further decisions on it are likely to be taken up only after the elections’

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026 | UDF united and ready for polls: Ramesh Chennithala
content: Says CPI(M) facing unprecedented crisis as leaders quit party; accuses government of last-minute decisions to mislead voters

title: Police nab all ten suspects in ‘Aluva Athul’ murder case
content: The arrests came within hours of the crime, following a massive manhunt across Kollam, Thiruvananthapuram, Pathanamthitta, and Idukki districts

title: Kerala Assembly Polls 2026: CPI declares candidates for Assembly polls in Kerala
content: Party has renominated all four of its incumbent Ministers, K. Rajan (Ollur), P. Prasad (Cherthala), J. Chinchurani (Chadayamangalam) and G.R. Anil (Nedumangad)

title: Three-year-old leopardess dies in road accident
content: The incident is said to have occurred at around 1 a.m. while the leopardess tried to cross the road on the stretch between Bannerghatta and the Kanakapura exit.

title: Three years on, justice eludes 2 Kuki-Zo women who were stripped, paraded, gang-raped in Manipur
content: Three accused in the case remain absconding, two are out on bail, and the bail application of another will come up for hearing in the Supreme Court later this month

title: Merit Day celebrated at St. Stephen’s College, Pathanapuram
content: A major highlight of the day was the formal unveiling of a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) between the college’s Post Graduate Department of Botany and the KSCSTE-Centre of Excellence in Microbiome

title: Sringeri pontiff inaugurates Veda Sabha at National Sanskrit University
content: Sri Vidhusekhara Bharathi Swamy calls upon students to spread Dharmic knowledge among the public and promote Indian spiritual values for global well-being

title: Over 6.4 lakh students to write SSC public examinations from March 16
content: The examinations will be held from 9.30 a.m. to 12.45 p.m. and students must report at the examination centre by 8.30 a.m., and entry will not be permitted after 10 a.m., says Director of Government Examinations Srinivasulu Reddy

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Edamalakkudy in spotlight at Election Commission’s briefing

title: Kanaka Durga temple officials plan restoration of ancient Pasupateswara temple on Indrakeeladri

title: Pak terrorist killed in Uri, infiltration bid foiled
content:
.

SRINAGAR: Army and police foiled an infiltration bid along LoC in J&K’s Uri sector, killing a Pakistani terrorist early Sunday. “Based on specific intelligence input provided by J&K police, a joint operation was launched. When challenged, the terrorist opened indiscriminate fire, prompting security forces to return fire,” Srinagar-based Chinar Corps of Army said.

Infiltration Bid Foiled In J&K: Pakistani Terrorist Killed In Army-Police Joint Operation In Uri

An AK rifle, pistols, and a large cache of ammunition were recovered from his possession. Army said the operation is continuing in the area, with security forces carrying out extensive searches to ensure no other infiltrators are present.

End of Article
